content.problemStatement:
Dr. Strange asks for your help and we don't have time for a background chit-chat!

NNN asteroids of different sizes are moving on a line, with the iii-th asteroid being in the iii-th coordinate. For each asteroid you know its direction (left or right), and its size. In this universe, all asteroids are moving with the same speed.

When two asteroids hit, there are two possible scenarios:


If they are the same size - they are both destroyed.
Otherwise, the smaller one is destroyed and the bigger one gains bonus size. The size of the bigger asteroid increases by the size of the smaller asteroid.
Help Dr. Strange determine which of the asteroids will survive (or if all of them will be destroyed).


Input Format

First line will contain TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow - each test case has N+1N+1N+1 lines.
The first line of input of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
Next NNN lines contain two space-separated integers: diridir_idiri​ and aia_iai​ - representing the direction (000 if left, 111 if right) and the size of the asteroid.


Output Format
For each test case, first output in a single line the number of survived asteroids - xxx.
In the next line, output xxx integers representing the indices of the survived asteroids sorted by increasing order\textbf{sorted by increasing order}sorted by increasing order.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105
10 points : diri=1dir_i = 1diri​=1 for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N
40 points : Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2000

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the first test case, the first two asteroids destroy each other, as they are the same size. Same thing happens to third and fourth asteroids - so all of them are destroyed and we are left with 000 asteroids.
In the second test case, the second and third asteroid collide and the bigger is third, so it now becomes larger and its size is 1+2=31+2=31+2=3. Next, the first asteroid collides with the asteroid of size 333 and wins. We are left with the first asteroid, whose size is 5+3=85+3=85+3=8 now, and the fourth asteroid with size 111.

Explanation:
The third asteroid hits the fourth and loses, so the fourth now has the size 5+3=85+3=85+3=8. Then, it hits the second asteroid and wins. So its size now is 8+2=108+2 = 108+2=10. Finally, the first asteroid is being destroyed by the fourth (whose size now is 10+9=1910+9 = 1910+9=19) and the fourth asteroid remains the only one.
